answers.value: Mechanical Engineering with a focus on sustainable energy systems

answers.value: I chose The Ohio State University because it offers a highly regarded, ABET-accredited Mechanical Engineering program with strong ties to local industry, including several companies where I have already completed job-shadowing experiences through my high school STEM academy.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: As someone who grew up in Columbus, I have spent years attending outreach events on OSU’s campus—such as the Engineering Open House and the annual Solar Decathlon presentations—which showed me firsthand how the university supports undergraduate research, hands-on projects, and collaboration between engineering students and faculty on real-world problems like improving energy efficiency in low-income housing.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: My long-term goal is to work on sustainable energy solutions, particularly affordable residential solar and improved battery storage; OSU’s dedicated research labs in energy systems, combined with co-op opportunities in the region and the ability to minor in Environmental Engineering, make it the best fit for my academic interests, my financial realities as a first-generation college student on my father’s side, and my desire to stay relatively close to home so I can continue supporting my younger sister and helping my dad manage our household responsibilities.
